skill.name: lark-note
skill.description: 飞书会议纪要（Note）直查：已知 note_id 时查询纪要详情、展示类型、关联文档 token，并读取 unified 原始逐字记录。当用户已持有 note_id，或从文档显式 vc-node-id 获得 note_id 时使用。不负责会议/日程/妙记定位、文档标题搜索或 Docx 正文读取。
skill.summary: Look up Lark meeting notes by note_id. Returns note type, document tokens, and unified transcripts.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only Markdown documentation files describing CLI command routing for Lark/Feishu meeting note lookups. The 112 static findings are all false positives caused by regex pattern matching on backtick characters in code formatting (e.g., `lark-cli note +detail`) and relative documentation links (e.g., `../lark-doc/SKILL.md`). No executable code, no network requests, no data exfiltration patterns, and no malicious intent detected.

content.user_title: Look up Lark meeting notes by note_id
content.value_statement: This skill retrieves Lark meeting note details and transcripts when you already have the note_id. It avoids searching or guessing note IDs, ensuring you get accurate, verifiable results from official API calls.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve meeting note details by explicit note_id using `lark-cli note +detail`
Display the note_display_type (unknown, normal, or unified)
Read unified transcript data via `lark-cli note +transcript`
Route to appropriate follow-up commands based on note type
limitations: Requires an explicit note_id; cannot search by title or reverse-engineer from doc_token
Does not handle meeting_id, calendar_event_id, or minute_token lookups (use lark-vc instead)
Cannot read Docx document body content (delegated to lark-doc skill)
Only supports unified transcripts for the transcript command; normal type uses docs +fetch

use_cases.title: Look up meeting note details
use_cases.description: Given a note_id, retrieve the note's display type, document token, and related metadata.
use_cases.target_user: Users with a known note_id from a Lark document

use_cases.title: Fetch a unified transcript
use_cases.description: Retrieve the full transcript of a unified meeting note, saved to a local markdown file.
use_cases.target_user: Users who need verbatim records of unified meeting notes

use_cases.title: Route from document to note
use_cases.description: Extract vc-node-id from a Lark document's vc-transcribe-tab element and use it to query the note domain.
use_cases.target_user: Users navigating from a Lark Docx to its underlying meeting note

prompt_templates.title: Basic note lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the meeting note with note_id 1234567890.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user already has a note_id

prompt_templates.title: Transcript retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the unified transcript for note_id abc123 and save it locally.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user needs verbatim meeting records

prompt_templates.title: Route from document
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this Lark document, find the vc-transcribe-tab element, extract the vc-node-id, then look up the note details.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user provides a Docx URL instead of a note_id

prompt_templates.title: Determine note type
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the note_display_type for note_id xyz789 and tell me whether it's unified, normal, or unknown.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user wants to know which routing path applies

output: verbatim_doc_token: (empty or present depending on type)

output_examples.input: Fetch the unified transcript for note_id 7000000000000000000
output: Contains full transcript with speaker attribution and timestamps

best_practices: Always require an explicit note_id before invoking any command; never reverse-engineer from doc_token or title
Check note_display_type first to determine the correct routing path for transcripts
Use `--format json` for machine-readable output when integrating with other tools
anti_patterns: Do not guess note_id from document title, body content, or backlink references
Do not use `note +transcript` for normal-type notes; route to `docs +fetch` instead
Do not add `--overwrite` without explicit user confirmation

faq.question: Where do I get a note_id?
faq.answer: The user must provide it directly, or it comes from the vc-node-id attribute in a vc-transcribe-tab element in a Lark document.

faq.question: What is note_display_type?
faq.answer: It is a field returned by note +detail that indicates whether the note is unknown, normal, or unified. It determines how transcripts are routed.

faq.question: Can this skill search for notes by title?
faq.answer: No. This skill only works with explicit note_ids. Use lark-drive or lark-doc for title-based searches.

faq.question: How are transcripts saved?
faq.answer: By default, unified transcripts are saved to ./notes/{note_id}/unified_transcript.md. Use --transcript-format plain_text for .txt output.

faq.question: What if note +transcript is called on a non-unified note?
faq.answer: The CLI validates the type first and will not pull the transcript if the note is not unified. Use docs +fetch for normal-type notes.

faq.question: Is the lark-cli binary required?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata declares lark-cli as a required binary. Install it before using this skill.
